summary: Track remaining reusable follow-up work after repairing OpenVEX/BOM contracts, expanding strict Ruff and mypy preview allowlists, adding Windows smoke CI, and landing the main promotion-gate/runtime helper decomposition tranche.

observations.problem: The first promotion-gate split is done, but rule-registry wiring and decision-finalize flow still remain in the main orchestration module.
observations.why_it_matters: The external review identified large orchestration modules as the main maintainability risk. Extracting state and report assembly reduced the surface, but rule-reduction and finalize logic are still concentrated in the same runtime.
observations.automation_candidate: Continue the promotion gate split with dedicated rule-registry and finalize helpers, then add those new helper surfaces to the strict-preview allowlists.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-25 KST: the rule-registry and finalize split already landed via `promotion_gate_mechanism_rule_registry_runtime.py` and `promotion_gate_mechanism_finalize_runtime.py`. Remaining follow-up is maintenance-only: keep the decomposed helper surfaces on strict-preview allowlists as appropriate and trim the facade further only if future complexity reports still flag it.

observations.surface: ops/scripts and tests static quality gates
observations.problem: The repo now has a schema-backed structural complexity budget artifact, but the remaining gate needs to stay narrow because the full preview report still surfaces existing attention targets.
observations.why_it_matters: The review report flags complexity accumulation as the main long-term risk, and the current gates do not yet promote that signal to a first-class artifact.
observations.automation_candidate: Generate a deterministic complexity-budget report and add a narrow gate that compares file/function metrics for touched runtime surfaces.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-21 KST by removing the no-op target loop, adding explicit --target and --changed-files-manifest inputs, and wiring a no-op-by-default complexity-budget-touched-check into check-strict. Keep the full complexity-budget-check opt-in until existing attention targets are reduced.

observations.problem: Large scenario tests and fixture builders still combine public contract assertions, integration scenarios, and seed-vault construction in a few very large files.
observations.why_it_matters: The review report calls out test asset size as a maintainability risk; splitting these files is useful but should be isolated from runtime contract changes to avoid accidental coverage drift.
observations.automation_candidate: Extract fixture builders and scenario groups into focused modules while preserving marker tiers and existing assertion semantics.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-24 by splitting seed policy/schema and smoke-builder logic out of tests/minimal_vault_runtime.py into tests/minimal_vault_seed_core.py and tests/minimal_vault_seed_smoke.py, keeping seed_minimal_vault() as a thin compatibility wrapper, and adding the new helper modules to the structural complexity watchlist. Future follow-up can still split large mechanism review scenario files, but the shared fixture debt is now isolated behind dedicated helper modules.
